items.title: SA childcare centre closes for three months after 'very serious' allergy incident

children.content: South Australia's Education Standards Board says it has "serious concerns" about an Edge Early Learning centre after a child was taken to hospital following an allergen incident, with the centre directed to
close immediately for the second time this year.

gallery.altText: A childcare centre on a quiet street

thumbnail.altText: A childcare centre on a quiet street

items.title: SANFL avoids strike action after months of negotiations over pay

children.content: SANFL Players' Association members have voted for a memorandum of understanding with the state league amid an ongoing decline in players' pay.

image.altText: A man in a long sleeve top stands next to a red brick wall

items.title: 'Pink army' Thunderbirds fans flock to Rundle Mall

children.content: Hundreds of fans have flocked to Rundle Mall, dressed in pink, to welcome back the Adelaide Thunderbirds following an unforgettable grand final win.

image.altText: A group of netball players pose with hundreds of fans dressed in pink

items.title: Socceroos winger says One Nation monoculture debate 'bulls***

children.content: The Socceroos star says anyone "trying to divide" the community was "probably not Australian themselves".

gallery.altText: A close-up image of a young man wearing a cap and a hoodie, he has a serious expression and looks to his left

thumbnail.altText: A close-up image of a young man wearing a cap and a hoodie, he has a serious expression and looks to his left

items.title: Police traverse steep terrain to rescue lost teenagers in national park

children.content: Three teenagers were rescued by police after becoming lost in a national park in Adelaide's south.

image.altText: A green sign with the words Onkaparinga River National Park on it

items.title: Wildcards from Whyalla return to win grand prix after earlier heartbreak

children.content: A year after a devastating technical disqualification snatched victory from five South Australian teens at a hydrogen-powered engineering race, they returned as wildcard entrants to the 2026 H2 Grand Prix Finals in Switzerland.

image.altText: Four teenage boys holding an Australian flag with a backdrop saying H2 Grand Prix

items.title: Ryan Bowman's death leads to new voice for patients and carers

children.content: After Ryan Bowman died at the Mount Gambier Hospital, his family said their concerns over his deteriorating health went unheard.

image.altText: A shot of a memorial which has the date February 16 at the front.

items.title: SA government's promotion of Origin's concession scheme under attack

children.content: The state government is facing increasing criticism over an Origin Energy electricity offer that was promoted to pensioners in the lead-up to the state election, after the company introduced a new pricing structure for the 2026/2027 financial year.

image.altText: a pensioner speaks at a press conference next to two politicians

items.title: Somerton Man report 'very soon' but intrigue around case set to endure

children.content: The man who worked out the identity of the Somerton Man through a DNA match says SA Police has told him it is close to finishing its report for the coroner to finally officially put the 78-year mystery to rest.

gallery.altText: Black and white photo of the Somerton Man

thumbnail.altText: Black and white photo of the Somerton Man

items.title: From Call of Duty to Dreamtime: These designers are lighting up video games

children.content: As part of NAIDOC Week, we meet the Indigenous creatives who are re-shaping Australia's video game industry.

image.altText: Two men stand with black t-shirts, one man on the left is playing a VR headset.

items.title: Broken back prompts apprentice electrician to chase footy career

children.content: Rather than ending his promising sporting prospects, a fractured back has prompted Mount Gambier tall Jobe Janeway to chase his dream of playing in the AFL.

image.altText: Jobe Janeway runs out with the South Australian team for a game against the Allies in the national championships

items.title: Pay increase the final straw for struggling small businesses

children.content: Small businesses struggling with rising costs say a recent wage increase is the final straw. In one case, the owner of a corner store that has been running for 70 years decided to close the business.

image.altText: A woman standing outside a store with her hand on the front door with a closed sign

items.title: Power surge home to down Roos, rulings handed down for Suns-Pies melee

children.content: Port Adelaide completes a hard-fought 21-point win over North Melbourne, St Kilda runs riot against Essendon, and the AFL fines rather than bans Brayden Maynard and Touk Miller for making contact with an umpire.

image.altText: An AFL player points at another AFL player, both in dark blue

items.title: Highest ever pressure in Australia brings bitterly cold nights for south-east

children.content: Preliminary data shows the highest ever pressure in Australia was recorded in Tasmania on Monday morning.

gallery.altText: the yarra valley in victoria covered in thick frost

thumbnail.altText: the yarra valley in victoria covered in thick frost

avatar.alt: ABC meteorologist Tom Saunders smiling in profile photo

items.title: AFP confirms threat made against Indian Prime Minister Narendra Modi

children.content: Australian Federal Police says a young person was identified and issued a formal warning over an online threat made towards Mr Modi, ahead of his official visit later this week.

image.altText: Man wearing suit shakes hands with a second man, standing in front of a wall with two different flags on it.

items.title: 'Disgusting' antisemitic graffiti sprayed on Anzac landmark

children.content: Swastikas and anti-Jewish abuse were discovered spray-painted on rocks and the historic Plantagenet Battery along Western Australia's south coast.

image.altText: 'Heil hitler' and a swastika sprayed on a wall of a historic bunker in Albany.

items.title: England's Azteca thriller a stunning deliverance from ghosts of the past

children.content: England teams of old would have buckled under the pressure put upon them by Mexico and their 80,000-strong band of supporters at the Azteca. But this England team turned a nightmare into a dreamland, at a venue haunted by so many ghosts of the past.

articles.title: Belgium slams FIFA decision after Trump intervenes for US star

children.content: US striker Folarin Balogun is free to play Belgium despite earning a red card in his last match, in a shock decision that delights Donald Trump and "astonishes" the Belgians.

image.altText: Folarin Balogun points to his forehead in celebration

articles.title: Quick hits: England star hurt in celebrations, Haaland surprises himself

children.content: England revels in a famous victory over Mexico, but the celebrations come at a cost for one star, while Erling Haaland's brilliance even has the man himself dumbfounded. Here are today's World Cup quick hits.

image.altText: Erling Haaland, Jordan Pickford, Jude Bellingham

articles.title: Ice-cold Haaland triumphs again at World Cup, Brazil with a long road back

children.content: Brazil has long been trying to re-capture its glory days, but they are fading further into obscurity, while Erling Haaland proves Norway is a genuine World Cup threat in 2-1 win.

image.altText: Erling Haaland looks tough in celebration of a goal

gallery.altText: Harry Kane and Jude Bellingham celebrate an England goal

thumbnail.altText: Harry Kane and Jude Bellingham celebrate an England goal

items.title: 'I was feeling threatened': Men removed from Labor conference demand answers

children.content: As Premier Chris Minns walked into the Sydney Town Hall, greeting delegates ahead of his speech, two men unfurled a Palestinian flag from the second-floor balcony before being escorted from the event by police.

image.altText: a man being removed from by police from NSW State Labor Party Conference on Saturda

items.title: Australia enters new alliance with Fiji and more Pacific nations could join

children.content: Australia and Fiji strike a new defence alliance in a major development that could reshape the Pacific's strategic landscape.

image.altText: Three seated men hold hands, the two on the sides holding documents in green folders.

items.title: 'Like a small iceberg': Rare whale calf spotted swimming off east coast

children.content: The calf, thought to be one of only a dozen in the Southern Hemisphere, has been spotted swimming alongside its mother.

image.altText: A view from above showing a whale calf swimming alongside its mother.

gallery.altText: A view from above showing a whale calf swimming alongside its mother.

thumbnail.altText: A view from above showing a whale calf swimming alongside its mother.

items.title: Concerns about cricket pitches on ovals after Melbourne football tragedy

children.content: Nathan Fitzgerald's club has asked authorities to look at the way hard cricket pitches are covered on multi-use ovals as the 27-year-old continues to receive end-of-life care.

image.altText: A football club's image of a 27-year-old on a blue background.

items.title: PM apologises for saying he would 'shag' Kylie Minogue in podcast game

children.content: Anthony Albanese made the comments during a game of "shag, marry, date" on the Bush Deep podcast on Friday, which have been widely condemned as inappropriate.

image.altText: A man and a woman in a composite image sit opposite to each other on a couch talking.

items.title: This Place: Thaaklatjika Mingkana

children.content: Mutawintji National Park is an area rich in Aboriginal culture, painting and wildlife, and where the red desert is now green and lush.
As part of NAIDOC Week the ABC is looking at Indigenous peoples' connection to country, and what makes it so special.
NAIDOC Week is marking 50 years of Deadly and runs from July 5 until July 12.

image.altText: A woman in Indigenous patterned shirt sits alongside a creek

gallery.altText: A woman in Indigenous patterned shirt sits alongside a creek

thumbnail.altText: A woman in Indigenous patterned shirt sits alongside a creek

items.title: Love a self-saucing pudding? These four are delicious and easy

children.content: You'd never guess it but self-saucing puddings are so easy to make. Here are four of our best for you try while the weather is cool.

image.altText: A plate of self-saucing sticky date and butterscotch pudding, served with vanilla ice cream, an easy hot dessert

items.title: Is a full day of screen time for kids ever OK?

children.content: Screen time has become a phrase that makes "parents feel instantly guilty", but how detrimental is a full day of screens on the odd occasion, like during school holidays or when you are home sick?

image.altText: little girl sitting on ottoman watching television

items.title: 'Oldest continually practised medicine' being passed on to students

children.content: Aboriginal Australians have used native plants as bush medicine for tens of thousands of years. On Larrakia land, medical students are learning how nature can heal.

articles.title: How NAIDOC grew from a one-day protest to a week-long celebration

children.content: Traditionally, NAIDOC Week was celebrated by First Nations people, but it has become an invitation for non-Indigenous Australians to get involved.

image.altText: A montage of images of NAIDOC protests and image of Day of Mourning with Indigenous leaders in 1938

articles.title: As NAIDOC turns 50, what is 'deadly' and what does it mean to you?

children.content: As official NAIDOC celebrations get underway across the country, the ABC's Matt Francis asks what is deadly and what does it mean to the Indigenous community across the country.

items.title: Hobart deputy lord mayor under fire over taking office snacks

children.content: Despite her apology and the integrity commission dismissing the case, Hobart's deputy mayor is still facing backlash after she took snacks from council premises for her home office.

items.title: Alice Springs tourism operators celebrate funding after toughest days

children.content: After several challenging years for tourism operators in central Australia, there is hope an incentive of free flights for visitors and a new grant fund will supercharge tourism in the region.

items.title: Council demands illegally cleared Sydney land be restored despite secret sale

children.content: In 2020, Amir Abu Abara illegally cleared land in Barden Ridge to build a lavish mansion. After a fine, a replanting works order, and the rejection of his housing plan, he is no longer the site's owner.

image.altText: Graphic showing the mansion in Barden Ridge with a pool

items.title: Billions in cash can't be accounted for. Where is it all going?

children.content: There is no shortage of theories, from stacks of cash hidden at home to illicit activities, but little in the way of definitive answers.

image.altText: An illustrated closed briefcase with $100 notes sticking out the sides

items.title: Reporting on Venezuela's disaster when we heard: 'Call your embassy right now'

children.content: Instead of focusing all its efforts on coordinating a widescale rescue operation, the Venezuelan government has been accused of hindering the response, and as has become apparent, our ability to cover it.

image.altText: A man crying while sitting on a wall.

items.title: The yellow blocks creating a 'game of cat and mouse' in Gaza

children.content: The "Yellow Line" that marks the boundary of Israeli-controlled territory in Gaza has been moved deeper into the enclave.

image.altText: Three photos of large concrete blocks painted yellow with signs, seen in front
